differences.title: 1. COMPILATION STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Svelte compiles components into imperative vanilla JS at build time, no runtime overhead.
differences.rightDesc: Vue uses a Virtual DOM for efficient updates and rendering, with a reactive runtime.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY APPROACH
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's reactivity is based on simple assignment statements, no special functions needed.
differences.rightDesc: Vue's reactivity uses JavaScript Proxies for tracking changes and updating the UI.

differences.title: 3. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte often results in smaller production bundles due to compile-time optimizations.
differences.rightDesc: Vue offers excellent runtime performance with its optimized Virtual DOM.

differences.title: 4. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's syntax is close to plain HTML, CSS, and JS, making it intuitive.
differences.rightDesc: Vue's concepts like components, directives, and lifecycle hooks are familiar to many developers.

differences.title: 5. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Svelte has a simple, built-in store system for managing application state.
differences.rightDesc: Vue relies on its ecosystem for state management (e.g., Pinia, Vuex).

differences.title: 6. TOOLING & ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Svelte's ecosystem is rapidly expanding with new libraries and tools.
differences.rightDesc: Vue boasts a mature and extensive ecosystem with robust community support.

differences.title: 7. TYPICAL USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Svelte excels in creating highly performant, lightweight components and apps.
differences.rightDesc: Vue is versatile, ideal for Single Page Applications and complex UIs.
